display.purpose: Shoutable words only: verdicts, versus names, taglines, TV podium.

shadows.name: cta-scroll-fade
shadows.purpose: Under the sticky CTA: signals content scrolling beneath. One of only two shadows in the system.

shadows.name: crown-drop
shadows.purpose: TV crown sitting above the podium.

components.name: CTA Button
components.description: The One Action: exactly one per screen, sticky in thumb reach.
components.css: .ds-cta { background: #f5ae39; color: #031e29; font-family: 'Bricolage', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 22px; min-height: 60px; padding: 0 24px; border: none; border-radius: 24px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 120ms, background 120ms; } .ds-cta:active { transform: translateY(2px) scale(0.99); background: #d98b09; } .ds-cta:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #f5ae39; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Hold-to-Talk Button
components.description: The signature control: 96px marigold circle that breathes with live mic level while a ring timer draws around it.
components.html: <button class="ds-talk" aria-label="Hold to talk"><svg width="44" height="44" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none"><rect x="9" y="2.5" width="6" height="11.5" rx="3" fill="currentColor"/><path d="M4.5 11a7.5 7.5 0 0 0 15 0" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.2" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M12 18.5V22" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.2" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-talk { width: 96px; height: 96px; border-radius: 50%; background: #f5ae39; color: #031e29; border: none; display: grid; place-items: center; cursor: pointer; touch-action: none; transition: transform 120ms, background 120ms; } .ds-talk:active { background: #d98b09; transform: scale(1.06); } .ds-talk:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #f5ae39; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Language Chip
components.description: Dual-script selectable chip; selected state gets a marigold border on a raised fill.
components.css: .ds-chip { min-height: 52px; border-radius: 10px; background: #003b40; border: 2px solid #225c60; color: #ecf6f5; display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 1px; padding: 4px 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 120ms, background 120ms, border-color 120ms; } .ds-chip:active { transform: scale(0.95); } .ds-chip-on { background: #004c50; border-color: #f5ae39; } .ds-chip-en { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; } .ds-chip-native { font-size: 14px; color: #a5c5c5; } .ds-chip-on .ds-chip-native { color: #ecf6f5; }

components.name: Answer Tile
components.description: Fat family-colored answer tile with full-size Indic label; lockout state desaturates.
components.css: .ds-tile { min-height: 76px; border-radius: 16px; background: #b5226c; color: #ecf6f5; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.3; padding: 12px 16px; border: none; text-align: center; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 120ms, filter 240ms, opacity 240ms; } .ds-tile:active { transform: scale(0.96); } .ds-tile:disabled { filter: saturate(0.15) brightness(0.7); opacity: 0.6; cursor: default; } .ds-tile:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #f5ae39; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Surface-filled field; focus swaps the border to marigold (no double ring).
components.css: .ds-input { background: #003b40; border: 2px solid #225c60; border-radius: 16px; padding: 12px 16px; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 700; color: #ecf6f5; transition: border-color 120ms; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #f5ae39; }

components.name: Leaderboard Row
components.description: Ranked surface row (never a card); the player's own row gets a marigold border.
components.html: 4Chai Champion180
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; background: #003b40; border-radius: 10px; padding: 12px 16px; color: #ecf6f5; } .ds-row-you { border: 2px solid #f5ae39; background: #004c50; } .ds-row-rank { font-family: 'Bricolage', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 22px; color: #a5c5c5; min-width: 1.6em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-row-name { flex: 1; font-weight: 700; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-row-score { font-family: 'Bricolage', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 22px; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Toast
components.description: Top-anchored status toast (never covers the talk button); shake variant for re-records.
components.css: .ds-toast { background: #004c50; border: 1px solid #225c60; color: #ecf6f5; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; padding: 12px 16px; border-radius: 16px; max-width: 420px; text-align: center; }

components.name: Warm Failure Panel
components.description: The No-Red Rule made concrete: failure states live on a warm dim surface with playful copy.
components.html:
We can’t hear you yet

Your voice is the whole game — without the mic there’s nothing to play.
components.css: .ds-fail { background: #552b17; border: 2px solid #b3621e; border-radius: 24px; padding: 32px 24px; color: #ecf6f5; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; } .ds-fail-title { font-family: 'Bricolage', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 28px; margin: 0; } .ds-fail-body { margin: 0; font-size: 18px; line-height: 1.5; }

narrative.overview: A game stall at an Indian festival, rebuilt as an app: a deep peacock-teal night-market world lit by one marigold action color and four saturated answer-tile hues, with a chunky display face doing the shouting and the system sans doing the talking. Kahoot's fat answer tiles, Duolingo's juicy feedback and blame-free misses, HQ Trivia's live-event tension — grounded in an unmistakably Indian palette. The system now exists as working code: every value in this document is extracted from web/src/styles/, and every color pair was contrast-verified numerically before it shipped.
keyCharacteristics: The peacock world is constant; marigold appears exactly once per screen, on the primary action
Four answer-tile hues, always paired with position and full-size text
Fixed rem type scale (1.2 ratio); Bricolage 800 for shoutable words, system sans for everything else; Indic game content in system Noto ≥24px, never restyled
Motion is choreographed at the beats (versus reveal, verdict flood) and instant everywhere else; everything that animates in settles

rules.name: The One Action Rule
rules.body: Marigold appears on exactly one element per screen: the primary action. Screens with no action have no marigold control — its absence is what makes the celebration floods land.

rules.name: The Never-Alone Rule
rules.body: Tile colors are always paired with position and a full-size text label. Color celebrates; it never carries meaning alone.

rules.name: The No-Red Rule
rules.body: Failure is warm, not alarming: the failure surface pair, playful copy, zero ❌ iconography anywhere in the app.

rules.name: The Arm's-Length Rule
rules.body: If a player standing in a noisy hall can't read it at arm's length, it's too small. Body never drops below 18px; game content never below 24px.

rules.name: The Settle Rule
rules.body: Everything that animates in visibly lands — rings stop, tiles settle, banners rest. Floating at rest is forbidden. Every animation has a reduced-motion fallback.

dos: Do keep exactly one marigold action per screen (The One Action Rule) — and zero on screens with nothing to press.
Do pair every tile color with position and a full-size label (The Never-Alone Rule).
Do render Indic game content in system Noto at ≥24px, everywhere, unstyled by the display face.
Do keep pointer-events: none on every decorative SVG overlay (rings) — a ring that eats a tap already shipped as a bug once.
Do verify contrast numerically when adding colors: body ≥4.5:1, large ≥3:1, and text-on-fill pairs checked against the ramp.
Do write failure states warm and playful on the Failure Surface pair — re-records tease, never blame.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback and let everything settle (The Settle Rule).
donts: Don't use generic AI-startup slop: no purple-blue gradients, no glassmorphism, no sparkle emojis, no "AI magic" framing.
Don't go childish edtech: no mascots, no primary-school aesthetics — players are adults at a tech event.
Don't build corporate dashboard surfaces: no gray cards, tiny type, or data density in the player's face; factory telemetry stays off the TV ticker.
Don't default to Western stock-photo minimalism — the culturally rooted card imagery is the only imagery.
Don't let the card label exist anywhere — DOM, hidden field, cached state — before the server's confirm-phase payload.
Don't use red for any failure state, or ❌ iconography anywhere.
Don't animate anything that blocks input on a mid-range Android; beats cap at 2.5s and are always tap-skippable.
